CVE-2010-1192.title: libESMTP, probably 1.0.4 and earlier, does not properly handle a  ...
CVE-2010-1192.description: libESMTP, probably 1.0.4 and earlier, does not properly handle a '\0' character in a domain name in the subject's Common Name (CN) field of an X.509 certificate, which allows man-in-the-middle attackers to spoof arbitrary SSL servers via a crafted certificate issued by a legitimate Certification Authority, a related issue to CVE-2009-2408.

CVE-2010-1194.title: The match_component function in smtp-tls.c in libESMTP 1.0.3.r1,  ...
CVE-2010-1194.description: The match_component function in smtp-tls.c in libESMTP 1.0.3.r1, and possibly other versions including 1.0.4, treats two strings as equal if one is a substring of the other, which allows remote attackers to spoof trusted certificates via a crafted subjectAltName.
